what_it_does: This file is a design reconciliation document: it takes nine coverage reports as viewpoints on one system and writes a single union map that inventories address schemes, registries, typed-edge systems, storage shapes, instrument rendering, duplication clusters, and architectural seams, then proposes unifications into 'one address space', 'one registry-of-registries', and 'one typed-edge law'.
observations: Declares 16 address schemes in contracts/address.py:SCHEMES and notes channel:// and cluster:// are used but absent from the tuple.
Lists roughly 25 file-discovered registries that share one os.listdir/importlib/fail-loud/id==stem pattern, plus two outliers (CapabilityRegistry, UI_REGISTRY).
Identifies hardcoded vocabularies that are not yet registries: SCHEMES literal, resolve_address if/elif chain, EDGE_KINDS dict, port-type strings.

Proposes three 'spines': one address grammar (granularity as a field on each scheme row), one registry-of-registries (FileDiscoveredRegistry base plus extended _CORPUS_REGISTRIES), one typed-edge law (verb with equal opposite).
Describes four current storage shapes (per-noun JSON dirs, append-only JSONL leaves, CAS blobs, vectors) and a future Supabase migration with prerequisites.
Maps the universal projection/instrument (wheel/lattice) as configured by BindingRegistry and ProjectionRegistry rows, not hardcoded layout.

Clusters duplication: copied registry mechanism, parallel comment/annotation stores, two channel registries, two mail logs, dual recall indexes, triple point-in-time primitives, triple capability inventory.
Notes built-but-unwired components: lifters, forms, model_routing.resolve_model, up_translate finding explainer, coherence gates.
Highlights a single hinge: recognisers-as-data via RULE_OPS AST to make triggers, forms, and lifters agent-authorable through one gate.
Notes a structured-output gap where cc_launch --json-schema output is dropped by session_supervisor._turn_done.
standouts: Granularity is treated as a field on each address-scheme row, subsuming node-instance, content-hash, UI-element, vector, capability-sub-file, etc. into one grammar.
The proposal to collapse ~25 copied registry loops into one FileDiscoveredRegistry base class and an enumerable _CORPUS_REGISTRIES table.
The 'one crux' insight that forms, lifters, and triggers are blocked from Tier-B authoring because their recognisers are Python callables, and the fix is RULE_OPS data-AST.
The storage swap-seam gap: Resolver Protocol has 10 methods, FsStore has 70, and Suite uses FsStore concretely.
Three disconnected comment/annotation stores for the same user act (board comment, canvas annotation, surface mark).
conventions: Follows a registry-per-file convention: one file = one row, id == filename, FIELDS validation, discover()/rediscover().
Follows fail-loud validation on missing FIELDS.
Follows a resolver dispatch pattern, currently as an if/elif chain.
Breaks the registry convention by keeping SCHEMES, EDGE_KINDS, port-types, and resolve_address as hardcoded code literals instead of registry rows.
Breaks single-source-of-truth by maintaining parallel stores for comments, channels, mail, recall, and ui:// addresses.
Uses internal shorthand and project-specific names (e.g., Tim's law, RHM, cc_launch, DNA substrate) without inline glossary.
concerns: The file asserts a 'ground truth' of 16 schemes and dismisses reports of 6/8/14, but does not show the verification evidence inside the file.
It mixes reconciliation, architectural prescription, bug inventory, and migration planning in one document, which may drift from implementation.
Proposed merges (Skill≡Context, board_edges≡relation_types) are stated as structural duplicates without proving semantic compatibility.
The Supabase migration plan depends on absorbing bypass namespaces and inline duplication that are only named, not detailed.
The 'one sentence' summary compresses many unresolved gaps into a claim of unity that may be aspirational.
notes: A dense reconciliation-and-unification brief that tries to collapse a patchwork of schemes, registries, edges, stores, and surfaces into one self-describing addressed graph.
questions: What is the exact verification that produced the count of 16 schemes versus the 6/8/14 reports?
How does FileDiscoveredRegistry handle registries with callable fields like lifters.extract or forms.match?
What is the precise grammar and operator set of the RULE_OPS data-AST?
How do the DNA substrate engine and the Company engine relate: is one an instance of the other?
How would CapabilityRegistry and UI_REGISTRY be represented in the extended _CORPUS_REGISTRIES without breaking the file-discovered count?
What is the intended migration order among Shape-A/B/C/D storage?
What does the _safe() address-to-filename transform do, and why does it belong in address.py?
What is 'Tim's law' / 'Tim's theorem' and where is it documented?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to reconcile nine coverage reports into one union map; the file does that and also acts as an architectural design brief, bug/gap inventory, and migration plan, so its scope is broader than pure reconciliation.
apparent_themes: One address space with granularity as a field
One registry-of-registries and a single discovery base
Typed-edge law: verb with equal opposite
Corpus (semantic) and substrate (structural) as dual lenses on the same units
Fusion of patchwork duplication rather than adding new systems
intention_signals: The document appears to be trying to establish a single authoritative model of the system, justify a corrected scheme count, and chart a path from a duplicated/patchwork codebase to one unified addressed graph where every mechanism is registry-declared and self-describing.
novelty: It is unusual to frame address schemes as rows carrying granularity and resolver status, to propose a single base class across ~25 heterogeneous registries, and to identify a single 'recogniser-as-data' hinge (RULE_OPS) that would simultaneously unlock triggers, forms, and lifters for agent authoring.
contribution: This file contributes a unified inventory of address schemes with resolver status and granularity fields; the concept of an enumerable registry-of-registries; an abstraction of a typed-edge law; a design where the instrument is configured by binding and projection rows; a catalog of duplication clusters and built-but-unwired components; and the insight that recognisers must be data-ASTs for Tier-B authoring.
summary_for_embedding: A design reconciliation document that maps one system's address schemes, registries, typed-edge systems, storage shapes, instrument rendering, duplication clusters, and seams into a proposed unified addressed graph.
intention_for_embedding: To reconcile nine coverage reports into a single union map and prescribe unification into one address grammar, one registry-of-registries, and one typed-edge law.
